channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SERAP Files Case Against FG Over Mass Phone Surveillance Rules
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don carry matter go ECOWAS Community Court of Justice against the Nigerian government under President Bola Tinubu because dem never withdra...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Deaths of Two Individuals Near Riverside Courthouse
item.description: Early Thursday morning, two individuals were found dead outside the Riverside County Courthouse in downtown Riverside, California. Riverside Police responded to reports of a possible stabbing at appro...

item.title: NMG's Enduring Legacy in Kenyan Journalism
item.description: The Nation Media Group (NMG) has been a pillar in Kenyan media for decades, standing tall through thick and thin. This opinion piece highlights how NMG has maintained its position as a trusted voice d...

item.title: Australian Government Orders Non-Essential Officials to Leave Israel and UAE Due to Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Australian government has instructed all non-essential officials to leave Israel and the United Arab Emirates, citing the worsening security situation in the Middle East following the US and Israe...

item.title: Empowering women entrepreneurs in Kenya's green economy
item.description: The shift towards a green economy is picking up speed globally, with sectors like renewable energy, climate-smart agriculture, and circular production gaining traction. However, women entrepreneurs in...

item.title: Odisha village gripped by anxiety as Iran-US-Israel war affects Gulf migrant workers
item.description: In Manikapur village of Ganjam district, Odisha, nearly every household has a family member working in Gulf countries. The ongoing war in West Asia between Iran and the US-Israel alliance, starting la...

item.title: Celebrating Eid Amid Global Conflict and Resilience
item.description: As the crescent moon signals the arrival of Eid-ul-Fitr, Muslims worldwide, including in South Africa, celebrate this important festival in the shadow of war and conflict. From Gaza to Tehran, million...

item.title: Tanzania to gain from Lifezone’s Burundi nickel agreement and SGR expansion
item.description: On 10th March 2026, Lifezone Metals, which owns Tembo Nickel, signed a 14-month exclusive deal with the Government of Burundi for the Musongati nickel deposit. The deposit, sitting about 200 km south-...

item.title: B.C. First Nations chiefs respond to MP Aaron Gunn's criticism of land acknowledgments
item.description: The chiefs of four First Nations in British Columbia — the Tla'amin, Homalco, K'omoks, and Klahoose — have responded to Conservative MP Aaron Gunn after he criticized land acknowledgments at public ev...

item.title: Prince and Princess of Wales Participate in Coffee and Cheese Activities at London Borough Market
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales made a lively appearance at London Borough Market, engaging directly with vendors and visitors. Princess Kate demonstrated her barista skills at the Change Please coff...

item.title: Canada announces $32B investment to strengthen Arctic defence infrastructure
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has unveiled a major $32 billion plan to upgrade military infrastructure in Canada's far north, enhancing the nation's ability to independently defend its Arctic territory. ...

item.title: Causes and Solutions for Kenya's Frequent Power Outages
item.description: Kenya has been facing frequent power cuts since late 2024, affecting millions of households and businesses across the country. These outages, often called load-shedding, happen mostly in the evenings ...

item.title: Knorr Expands Ramadan Iftar Program to Support Communities Across Nigeria
item.description: As Ramadan dey roll across Nigeria, Knorr don launch dem yearly 'Share The Good' programme, wey dey visit communities for Ogun, Oyo, and Lagos states first. The initiative na part of Knorr commitment ...

item.title: FlySafair Introduces Temporary Fuel Surcharge Amid Rising Jet Fuel Costs
item.description: FlySafair, South Africa's largest domestic airline, will implement a temporary fuel surcharge on all tickets for flights departing on or before 12 May 2026. The decision comes after a Middle East cris...

item.title: Civil Society Group Atin Ito Plans Fourth Mission to Deliver Aid to Pag-asa Island
item.description: Civil society coalition Atin Ito has announced plans for its fourth civilian mission to Pag-asa Island in April, under the theme 'Balik Pag-asa.' The mission aims to support Filipino fisherfolk and re...

item.title: Government Chemist Authority Supports 146,567 Jobs Through Chemicals Sector Expansion
item.description: From January 2025 to February 2026, the Government Chemist Laboratory Authority (GCLA) in Tanzania created over 146,567 jobs, most of which went to young people. This growth came through registration ...

item.title: US Aerial Refuelling Aircraft Crashes in Western Iraq, Second Plane Lands Safely
item.description: According to the US military, an American KC-135 aerial refuelling aircraft has crashed in western Iraq while a second plane involved in the operation managed to land safely. The US Central Command (C...

item.title: How to Make the Most of Travel Loyalty Points Without Losing Value
item.description: In the complex world of travel loyalty programs, hoarding points without a clear strategy often leads to disappointment. Loyalty points can lose value due to 'devaluations,' which occur when programs ...

item.title: Armed man killed after driving truck into Michigan synagogue, no injuries reported
item.description: An armed man drove a truck into the hallway of Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield Township near Detroit, Michigan, while children were attending the centre's early childhood program. The ve...

item.title: U.S. KC-135 Refuelling Aircraft Crashes in Western Iraq Amid Military Operations Against Iran
item.description: On 12th March 2026, a U.S. Air Force Boeing KC-135 Stratotanker, used for aerial refuelling, crashed in western Iraq. According to U.S. Central Command, the incident involved another aircraft, but it ...
